content:
# elizaos/eliza Weekly Report (Jun 14 - 20, 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
This week, the ElizaOS team achieved a major milestone in platform stability, successfully transitioning the Eliza Cloud Apps platform from staging to production. Development was heavily focused on hardening infrastructure, resolving critical billing and provisioning leaks, and optimizing agent scalability through architectural shifts like the adoption of in-container PGlite databases. The team also made significant strides in mobile parity, ensuring that Android and iOS builds are fully functional with bundled web assets, while simultaneously refining the developer experience through CI/CD workflow optimizations.

## 🛠️ Key Developments

### Cloud Infrastructure & Scaling
The team prioritized the reliability of the Eliza Cloud platform, moving from a centralized Postgres model to a more scalable architecture.
*   **Database & Provisioning:** To resolve "too many clients" errors and connection exhaustion, the team implemented local in-container PGlite databases for managed agents ([#8754](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8754)).
*   **Gateway & Routing:** BitRouter was decommissioned in favor of a Cloudflare Worker-based model gateway ([#8741](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8741)). Additionally, IaC for Headscale on the control plane was codified to ensure robust agent isolation ([#8565](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8565)).
*   **Billing Integrity:** Critical billing leaks were addressed by enforcing container stop-jobs and ensuring atomic credit-balance decrements ([#8517](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8517)).

### Mobile & UI/UX Enhancements
Significant effort was directed toward unifying the mobile and desktop experience.
*   **Mobile Pipeline:** Resolved long-standing issues with "web-less" APK builds by unifying Capacitor and Gradle build trees, ensuring all assets are correctly bundled ([#8387](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8387)).
*   **Chat & Onboarding:** Introduced a REST-based chat adapter for mobile ([#8527](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8527)) and standardized a floating chat surface across all views to improve discoverability ([#8597](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8597)).
*   **Onboarding:** Streamlined the first-run experience with a 3-way runtime chooser and passkey setup for cloud accounts ([#8469](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8469), [#8512](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8512)).

### CI/CD & Developer Experience
*   **Workflow Optimization:** Implemented path-based gating for expensive test lanes to streamline the PR process ([#8408](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8408)).
*   **Infrastructure:** Migrated trusted Linux CI jobs to self-hosted Hetzner runners to improve performance and reliability ([#8501](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8501)).

## 🐛 Issues & Triage

### Closed Issues
*   **Authentication:** Resolved production login failures for "Sign in with Eliza Cloud" by standardizing OAuth PKCE ([#8257](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8257)).
*   **Billing:** Successfully audited and closed all critical billing leak issues, ensuring profitability and correctness for the go-live phase ([#8342](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8342)).
*   **Runtime:** Closed the Runtime Open Federation issue, enabling permissionless task participation for Eliza agents ([#8345](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8345)).

### New & Active Issues
*   **UI Hijacking:** A new issue ([#8613](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8613)) regarding the `VIEWS` action hijacking text-connector turns was identified and addressed via connector-capability tiering.
*   **Launch Tracking:** The Cloud 10-user launch tracker ([#8434](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8434)) remained the central focus for the week, with the team successfully verifying the production cutover and provisioning chain.
*   **Future Work:** Infrastructure-level scaling, such as multi-node scaling and database-per-tenant sharding, remains on the roadmap as operator-level tasks.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
The week was characterized by high-intensity collaboration focused on the production launch. The successful resolution of the "web-less APK" regression and the "too many clients" database error involved significant cross-team coordination between infrastructure and mobile engineers. The community demonstrated strong engagement in the Cloud 10-user launch tracker, with active discussions ensuring that the staging-to-production cutover was handled with minimal disruption. The addition of third-party plugins like `elizaos-plugin-reddit-search` ([#8347](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8347)) highlights the growing ecosystem around the Eliza framework.
